mnemonics: RITA Runs In Threads Always (Runnable, Interrupt, Thread, Alive)
SHARE Carefully (Synchronization, Handling, Atomicity, Race conditions, Exceptions)
keyPoints: A thread can be created by extending Thread class or implementing Runnable interface
Synchronization is required to prevent race conditions when multiple threads access shared resources
The wait(), notify(), and notifyAll() methods are used for inter-thread communication
commonMistakes: Forgetting to call start() method (using run() instead which doesn't create a new thread)
Not properly synchronizing access to shared variables leading to race conditions
Creating too many threads which can lead to performance degradation due to context switching
